details.text: Derivative: إِذَا means “when” and indicates to something that happens. Therefore, in this context, its use tells us that women will be divorced as a reality and it is a matter in life that is bound to happen. Also, when إِذَا is followed by a فِعْل الماضِي (Verb in the past form), it can indicate to an action that you are about to do in certain constructs which include an instruction. As such it can be an instruction for preparation when you are about to do something i.e., to have the knowledge about how to deal with a matter before it occurs. According to this understanding إِذَا can hold a meaning similar to “if and when”. And example of this is His statement: فَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ “So, when you (intend to) recite the Qur'an, [first] seek refuge in Allah from Satan, the expelled” (An-Nahl: 98). The meaning here could therefore be: “If or when you want or intend to divorce your wives”.

Derivative: الْعِدَّةَ - عِدَّة (‘Iddah) comes from عَدَّ which is to count and عَدَد which is a number. It refers to the time from when the count starts and is used to refer to the time between periods or the length of the menstrual cycle.

Derivative: فَاحِشَةٍ refers to indecent or shameless acts or behaviour. It refers to an act of the utmost badness, indecency and hideousness and is often used to refer to Zinaa (adultery - fornication).
Derivative: مُبَيِّنَةٍ means to be absolutely clear.

- This Surah and the Surah that follows are unique in that they begin with: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ “O Prophet”. In this verse, it moves directly from addressing the Prophet ﷺ to addressing the believers as a collective using the plural طَلَّقْتُمُ (You (plural) have divorced). As such: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ “O Prophet” implies grabbing the attention of the Prophet ﷺ, like saying: “Listen O prophet, to what I am about to tell you”. By starting an address to the believers by beginning with an address to the Prophet ﷺ is also an indication to the high priority that the address should have with the believers.

- This means at the time of their عِدَّة (‘Iddah) which has been understood to mean the time of their purity i.e., they should be divorced when they are in a state of purity after their menses and not during their menses.

- يَا أَيُّهَا is نِداء التَّكْرِيم (A call or address to someone attributing honour to that person). It is also said that the أَيُّ is the حَرْفُ النِّداء (The word of address or call, meaning: “O” like for example: “O Zaid” or “O Ahmad” etc. And that the هَا is ها التَّنْبيه meaning that its purpose is to draw the attention i.e., “O Prophet, listen carefully”.
أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ refers to people of sound minds and those who can think clearly.

Derivative: رَسُولًا “A Messenger” follows as a connecting explanation for the preceding word found at the end of the previous verse which was ذِكْرًا (Dhikr) which is commonly understood to refer to the Qur’an when mentioned. Here a Messenger (رَسُولًا) is the explanation for ذِكْرًا, which indicates that the Qur’an and the Messenger ﷺ are inseparable. The Dhikr (reminder) is a Messenger who recites the clear verses upon you …
Derivative: مَعْرُوفٍ – Literally means: “Known” and refers to those standards which are well-known amongst everyone. بِمَعْرُوفٍ (Bi-Ma’ruf) means in a decent and honourable manner.

Derivative: بَالِغُ from بَلَغَ which means to reach. Here, it means Allah will certainly reach (بَالِغُ) (i.e., accomplish) His matter (أَمْرِهِ)” i.e., He will see His matter through to the end until He has accomplished it.

Derivative: تَعَاسَرْتُمْ is the plural second person form of تَعَاسَرَ comes from عُسْر difficulty, and it means to make things difficult between each other, or to give each other a hard time. In this context it means to be unable to reach an agreement amongst yourselves.
Derivative: أْتَمِرُوا from اِئتمرَ which means to confer and deliberate. اِئتمرَ implies that a back and forth and takes place before a decision is reached.
Derivative: تُضَيِّقُوا from ضَيَّقَ which means to make tight and to constrain. It means here to harass or to make things difficult for someone or something.
Derivative: نُّكْرًا refers to something that the mind can’t fathom due to how terrible it is.
Derivative: عَتَتْ from عَتَا which means to disobey out of pride, to be recalcitrant.
